mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive change in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient at p less than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns exhibit a negative or zero correlation with INDPRO growth rates across all tested rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 310, rising inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces tighter monetary policy expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL surpasses 310.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline shows no predictive power for 20-day returns with an F-stat p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0 percent during late-cycle expansions validates sustained aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns specifically during late-cycle phases, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below its 2017 baseline of 100, severe supply-side contraction signals imminent earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of observations where INDPRO drops below 100.
